title: European Commission and Europa Nostra spotlight Europe’s best heritage projects and champions at European cultural event in Nicosia
content:
Press release  | Photos | Video recording

The 30 winners of the 2026 European Heritage Awards / Europa Nostra Awards, the highest heritage accolade in Europe, were celebrated this evening during a high-profile event held at the Nicosia Municipal Theatre, Cyprus.

title: Europa Nostra presents a landmark Report on Partisan Memorial Cemetery in Mostar at the Parliamentary Assembly of Bosnia and Herzegovina and national TV channels
content: On 11 May, Sneška Quaedvlieg-Mihailović, Secretary General of Europa Nostra, presented a comprehensive Report urging for the Partisan Memorial Cemetery in Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina, to be revitalised, properly managed and made publicly accessible, at a high-level conference held in Sarajevo. The conference was hosted by MP Jasmin Imamović, on behalf of the Parliamentary Assembly of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was live broadcasted by the public TV of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

creator: Joana Pinheiro
sourceDescription: The Voice of Cultural Heritage in Europe

title: European experts call for urgent action to revitalise the endangered Partisan Memorial Cemetery in Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina
content:
Press release | Report
 
Europa Nostra, the leading European heritage civil society network, has released a comprehensive Report  urging for the Partisan Memorial Cemetery in Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina, to be revitalised, properly managed and made publicly accessible. The Report was prepared by a European team of experts following the inscription of this national monument on the list of the 7 Most Endangered Programme. This programme is run by Europa Nostra with the support of the European Investment Bank Institute and the Creativ

title: European experts implement mission to Armenia to assess urgent risks at the Arakelots Monastery and Settlement
content:
From 16 to 22 April 2026, European experts travelled to Armenia for a mission to the Arakelots Monastery and Settlement, listed among the 7 Most Endangered heritage sites in Europe for 2025. The site was nominated by the Armenian Studies Division, Centre for the Study of the Christian East, University of Salzburg, in recognition of its exceptional cultural significance and the urgent need to halt its ongoing deterioration, under the coordination of Prof. Dr. Jasmine Dum-Tragut, Professor of Armenian Studies and Director of the Centre.

title: Last Days to Register for the European Cultural Heritage Summit 2026 in Nicosia, Cyprus
content:
Europa Nostra and the Europa Nostra Heritage Hub in Nicosia are organising — with the sense of shared commitment and responsibility — the European Cultural Heritage Summit 2026 from 26 to 30 May in Nicosia, under the motto “Heritage as the Soul of Mare Nostrum”. This Summit is held under the patronage of the Cyprus Presidency of the Council of the EU, and is supported by the European Union.

We thank all who have already registered to attend our Summit in Nicosia. We also urge other members and partners who wish to join us in Nicosia to register as soon as possible, and in any case before 11 May.
snippet: Europa Nostra and the Europa Nostra Heritage Hub in Nicosia are organising — with the sense of shared commitment and responsibility — the European Cultural Heritage Summit 2026 from 26 to 30 May in Ni
creator: Joana Pinheiro
sourceDescription: The Voice of Cultural Heritage in Europe

title: Europa Nostra contributes to stakeholder consultation on the Culture Compass at the European Committee of the Regions
content:
On 23 April 2026, Europa Nostra took part in a stakeholder consultation organised by the European Committee of the Regions in the framework of its forthcoming opinion on the European Commission’s Culture Compass for Europe, which outlines key directions to guide EU policymaking in regards to the culture and creative sectors. The meeting, led by Rapporteur Tanya Hristova, Mayor of Gabrovo and Chair of the SEDEC Commission, brought together representatives of local and regional authorities, European networks, and cultural stakeholders to exchange views on the future of EU cultural policy.

© Europa Nostra

 

A strategic moment for shaping the Culture Compass

title: Our member organisation Touring Club Italiano publishes monthly column dedicated to Europa Nostra
content:
Touring Club Italiano (TCI), an important Italian Member Organisation of Europa Nostra, has launched a column in its monthly magazine Touring dedicated to the synergies between the two organisations.

Touring is a 131-year-old Italian magazine distributed to the members of Touring Club Italiano and has a distribution of 175.000 copies.

Cristina Loglio, Vice-President of Europa Nostra, is the author of this monthly column in the Italian magazine that aims to showcase the achievements of the joint efforts of the organisations in a European light.

title: European Commission and Europa Nostra announce the winners of Europe’s top heritage awards 2026
content:
The press release in 16 languages, photos and videos can be found at the bottom of this webpage

The European Commission and Europa Nostra have announced the 2026 winners of the European Heritage Awards / Europa Nostra Awards, which are co-funded by the Creative Europe Programme of the EU. This year, Europe’s most prestigious awards for heritage go to 30 exemplary projects and champions from 18 countries throughout the continent (see the full list below).

creator: Joana Pinheiro
sourceDescription: The Voice of Cultural Heritage in Europe

title: European experts implement mission to Gothenburg to assess the challenges facing the Valhalla Swimming Hall Complex
content: From 16 to 21 March 2026, European experts travelled to Gothenburg, Sweden, for a mission related to the city’s Valhalla Swimming Hall Complex (Valhallabadet), listed among the 7 Most Endangered heritage sites in Europe for 2025, in the framework of the 7 Most Endangered Programme run by Europa Nostra with the support of the European Investment Bank Institute. In collaboration with representatives from Europa Nostra Sweden and the local heritage group FASAD, a series of meetings were held, aimed at mobilising local and national stakeholders for the preservation of this iconic building, winner of the 1948 Olympic Medal for Architectural designs.
